gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (version 1.0.0-seed) has every article marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. Articles I-VI covering Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules are all empty. This is the constitutional identity of the DIO — without it, Arbiter (#190) has no substantive perimeter to enforce, and the MSP's subordination clause ('strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights') references a void. The DIO is operating without a defined scope of enterprise or any prohibited activities.

The MSP Rules section explicitly states: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' Nomos (#200) has no rules, invariants, constraints, or safety rules to evaluate against Action Proposals. Every AP passes the MSP compliance gate by default. Combined with the empty BoR (GAP-001), both of Nomos's two compliance gates are effectively inoperative.

The MSP references Genome documents that should live under a `genome/` directory, extending the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. The seed contains none. Every organ (Senate, Arbiter, Nomos, Cerberus, Glia, Vigil, etc.) operates without organ-specific operational constraints or risk-tier boundaries.

The MSP specifies a detailed amendment protocol: PEMs issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked → Senate-Legislator drafts → review state → HOM notification to human-principal mailbox → POST /amend/review with decision=approve → Senate-Rollout executes staging→canary→activation→rollback pipeline. None of this has ever occurred. No PEM concepts exist in Graph. No `msp_version` beyond the seed has been registered. The entire governance amendment pipeline is theoretical.

Graph structural data shows 33 statute concepts and 19 ruling concepts registered. Cerberus has produced 1 audit. However, the BoR (the constitutional foundation upon which all statutes must rest) is an empty scaffold. This creates a legal hierarchy inversion — operational law exists without a constitution to validate it against. Cerberus is auditing rulings that have no constitutional basis.

The BoR can only be amended by a human principal ('Authority: human-principal-only'). The amendment protocol requires HOM notifications to a `human-principal` mailbox. The Hippocampus data shows `recent_conversations: []` — zero conversations recorded. Minder shows human peer 'Leon Cohen-Levy' last seen 2026-04-02 (31 days ago at snapshot time). The BoR remains in scaffold state because no human has authored it.

Spine state data shows every organ (Arbiter, Nomos, Senate, Cerberus, and 22+ others) oscillating between ALIVE and DEGRADED states in ~5-second cycles. The pattern is: mass DEGRADED (reason: 'missed_pong') → mass ALIVE (reason: 'pong_recovered') → repeat. This affects all governance organs equally. The heartbeat mechanism that monitors organ health is triggering false-positive degradation on every cycle, degrading to alive, then degrading again.

Radiant context shows a pattern of SafeVault backup failures spanning 2026-04-04 through 2026-05-03. The most recent (2026-05-03) was 'hung_then_killed' — sqlite3 backup blocked indefinitely by zombie processes from the previous day. NAS mount failures recurred on 2026-04-27, 2026-04-28, 2026-05-01. S3 was available but NAS (//10.10.10.50/agentic-safevault-ai-platform) failed repeatedly. The 2026-04-30 backup failed due to an unbound variable bug (ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS array). The constitutional documents (MSP, BoR, statutes, rulings) stored in Graph/spine.db are at risk if backups are unreliable.

The MSP describes Nomos (#200) as evaluating 'every Action Proposal adjudication' through two compliance gates. The Graph concept type distribution shows no `action_proposal` concept type. There are `authorization_token` concepts (13) and `llm_usage_event` concepts (948), but no evidence that any action has gone through the formal AP → Nomos adjudication → authorization flow. The governance machinery exists but has never processed its primary workload.

The MSP states 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory' and describes them as extending the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. The world state contains no reference to any genome/ directory, no genome document concepts in Graph, and no organ-specific rule bindings. This is not just empty — the structural scaffolding for organ-specific governance doesn't appear to exist at all.

There are no defined objectives, invariants, or safety rules for memory consolidation, context freshness, embedding coverage, or dream-cycle cadence. Without autonomous dream-cycles, memory degrades, context bloats, and the platform's ability to provide a reliable world-model erodes.",

However, until MSP objectives exist, there is no machine-enforced trigger to fix this health gap."
